overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a robust multi-platform social media presence with a total of 3,580 followers. His content primarily revolves around computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. While LinkedIn commands the largest follower base, Bluesky shows the highest engagement rate per post, indicating a highly active and receptive audience there. Recent activity suggests a consistent output of high-value academic and professional updates.

contentThemes.description: Updates on new research, paper publications, algorithmic releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0), and discussions on methodological approaches to studying disinformation and coordinated behavior.
Announcements for grants awarded, hiring calls for postdoctoral researchers, and opportunities for PhD students in computational social science.
Mentions of upcoming conference presentations and participation in academic discussions.

recommendations: Prioritize Bluesky for announcing new research, papers, and calls for collaboration/opportunities, as it currently yields the highest engagement rate.
Continue to leverage LinkedIn for major professional announcements like grant awards and hiring opportunities, as it attracts a large and relevant professional audience.
Explore cross-posting and tailoring content across platforms. For instance, a research release on Bluesky could link to a more detailed professional announcement on LinkedIn.
Encourage more direct interaction by asking questions or inviting discussion in posts, particularly on Mastodon, to potentially boost its engagement closer to other platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), particularly for research publications and calls for opportunities. This platform serves as a strong channel for direct interaction and disseminating new academic findings to an engaged audience.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon shows a solid, albeit lower, engagement rate (4.55%) for research updates and methodological discussions. It appears to serve a dedicated, niche academic community, making it suitable for sharing detailed work and fostering intellectual exchange.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn, with the largest follower base, proves highly effective for professional announcements such as grant awards and hiring calls, achieving good engagement for these specific content types (average 4.87%). Its strength lies in reaching a broad professional and academic network for career-related news.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
